We moved ConditionalOnExpression from class to method so that once log level will be changed on fly, there should not be any need of restarting CPS service to take immidiate effect. If you will keep "ConditionalOnExpression" at class level than we have to restart CPS service to cteate CpsLoggingAspectService bean. To avoid service restart and make this bean available ConditionalOnExpression is moved to method. Now if we change log level on fly than immediatly the behaviour of this method will start working. In short for any log level change it will check the ConditionalOnExpression of fly and act accordingly.

This method logs time only if the method to be logged is executed successfully, but it does not log anything in the case of error. Is it expected?

I think this method is getting called even if ConditionalOnExpression is false.

To validate it, we can put an info log just to check if this method gets called when logging.level.org.onap.cps is other than 'DEBUG'. By default, the logger itself supports dynamic log level change using actuator loggers endpoint. So, we won't be able to check by just checking if log statements are being printed as it is being handled by the logger already.  

I validated it at my end and condition expression had no effect on method execution.
Issue-ID: CPS-604

the intention was to log timing for successful execution. I presume if the method being logged throws an exception or similar there still we be an execution time?
In any case erroe logging was not the intent of this user story...

Ack

In case of any exception we already capturing a proper exception and creating a service response for client.

Purpose : Don't want to capture or ignore in case of any exception as we already have ExceptionHandlers to do this otherwise (break the current flow of service and we will get unwanted/unstructured response that is useful for developer but not for end user) we need to have capture all kind of exception here and either throw it again or again generate service response as it's already done into ExceptionHandlers. If we capture any exception here (Stmt at line no. 57 throws Throwable) than we need to be wrap it to it's specific exception that is unnecessary. The @SneakyThrows annotation from Lombok allows us to throw checked exceptions without using the throws declaration that can be captured into ExceptionHandlers. @SneakyThrows is used to throw checked exceptions without declaring them in signature.
Done

Fixed (Major, Code smell) : Utility classes should not have public constructors

If access level is private than we were facing : IllegalArgumentException: No visible constructors in class NetworkCmProxyRestExceptionHandler. Basically in this case problem is with AOP proxy, for some reason CGLIB proxying not working in this case.
Fixed (Code smell, Minor) "throws" declarations should not be superfluous.
